start.text:
Welcome to MCR (Mahjong Competition Rules)! The main challenge of this variant is finding a way to score 8 Fan before everyone else.
(Click to continue)

Each round always starts with a flower declaration round. Starting from the East seat, each player replaces flowers in hand, or declares "No Flowers".
(Click to continue)

Declared flowers are worth 1 Fan, but do not count towards the required 8 Fan. Try declaring a flower now.
(Click to continue)
Now that you are out of flowers, press Pass to pass the turn.

In MCR, the goal is to obtain 8 Fan, and this is usually done by going for a high-scoring "main" Fan (6+ Fan) and adding some minor Fan (1-2 Fan) to make up the difference.
(Click to continue)

In this case, our hand is a really good mix of sequences in 3 suits. In this situation, it is common to go for Mixed Shifted Sequences (6 Fan if you have 123, 234, 345 in three suits) as our main Fan.
(Click to continue)
The player to our left has discarded a 6 of bamboo, which we'll need for Mixed Shifted, so go ahead and call it! In MCR, there is very little downside to calling tiles.
We'd also like to get All Sequences (2 Fan if all sequences, and no honor tiles), which would bring the total to the required 8 Fan. For this, we'll discard the wind tile.

You drew a flower! When you draw a flower in the middle of a game, you can either declare them (like at the start of the round) or discard them (to avoid dealing a dangerous tile).
(Click to continue)
It doesn't make sense to defend this early in MCR, so early flowers are always declared for a replacement tile. Do so now.

This draw (4 of circles) is ideal, not only because it lets us win on the 1 of characters (for Mixed Shifted 234 345 456)...
(Click to continue)

...but also on the 4 of characters (for Mixed Straight 123 456 789, worth 8 Fan by itself)! Switching tracks like this is quite common in MCR, since there are so many Fan patterns.
(Click to continue)
For this you'll need to discard the 2 of circles. Try it!
Obviously (since this is a tutorial) we get the 4 of characters immediately. Press Hu to see how this hand scores!

On top of our mixed straight, we get our two flower tiles, plus All Chows (all sequences plus non-honor pair), totalling 12 points.
(Click to continue)

If you win on a discard (like we just did) the discarder pays the 12 points, and on top of that, every player pays 8 points. This means an 8 Fan hand is worth 32 points at minimum.
(Click to continue)

If you had won by self-draw, everyone would pay you 12+8 points (plus one for 1 Fan Self-Drawn), totalling 63. It's worth a lot to self-draw!
(Click to continue)

Click the tabs on the left to see more patterns, or press Back on the upper right to return to the list of tutorials.
(Click to continue)
